name: Chip
description: Chips are compact elements that represent an input, a filter, or a choice — a selected driver, an active filter on a vehicle list, a quick action. They pack a lot of interaction into a small footprint. The karoo-ui wrapper applies no default-prop overrides itself; the app-wide `size="small"` default comes from the shared theme's MuiChip defaultProps, not this component.
keyRule: Use chips for selections and filters the operator manipulates. For read-only status (Active/Idle/Offline) map the tone with `color`/`sx` from the statusChip tokens — real MUI Chip has no native `status` variant.

variants.name: Filled
variants.whenToUse: `variant="filled"` (real MUI default) — the standard solid chip.

variants.name: Clickable / deletable
variants.whenToUse: Add `onClick` (clickable, shows a hover/press ripple) and/or `onDelete` (adds a trailing delete icon) to turn a static label into an interactive filter/input chip. There is no dedicated input/filter/choice/action prop — these are usage patterns built from the same Chip API.

variants.name: Status (read-only, usage pattern)
variants.whenToUse: Map record status (Active=success, Idle=warning, Offline=error) to `color`/`sx` using the statusChip tokens; keep it non-interactive (no onClick/onDelete).

anatomy.name: Container
anatomy.description: pill-shaped surface (16px radius); filled or outlined.

anatomy.name: icon / avatar
anatomy.description: optional leading `icon` or `avatar` element (e.g. a driver avatar).

anatomy.name: deleteIcon
anatomy.description: optional trailing remove control, shown automatically when `onDelete` is set.

behaviors.name: Clickable vs static
behaviors.description: Passing `onClick` (or `clickable`) makes the chip focusable and shows press/hover feedback; without it, Chip renders as a static, non-interactive label.

behaviors.name: Removal
behaviors.description: Setting `onDelete` shows a trailing delete icon; clicking it fires the callback (Backspace/Delete also work when the chip is focused).

behaviors.name: Selection (usage pattern)
behaviors.description: There is no built-in `selected` prop — filter/choice behaviour is built by the consumer toggling `color`/`variant` in response to local or controlled state.

behaviors.name: Overflow
behaviors.description: When many chips are present, wrap them in a flex container that wraps to multiple lines, or provide a "+N more" affordance yourself — Chip has no built-in overflow handling.

states.treatment: State-layer overlay, only when `onClick`/`clickable` is set.

specs.value: 24px (size="small" — the app-wide theme default); 32px (size="medium", real MUI default)
16px pill (semantic.radius.chip — MUI's own Chip default, not theme.shape's 4px)
Roboto 13px/400 (small) or 14px/400 (medium); status-chip usage commonly steps down to 12px/500 (semantic.typography.scale.labelSmall) via sx
~8-12px sides depending on size; MUI's built-in spacing, not separately tokenized
50-tone background + 900-tone text per status colour (semantic.color.statusChip.*) — applied via sx, not a native variant

props.name: label
props.description: The value or option text. Keep labels short.

props.name: color
props.description: Default 'default' (grey). Drives the status-mapping usage pattern.

props.name: size
props.description: Real MUI default is 'medium'; the shared Cartrack theme sets the app-wide default to 'small' via MuiChip defaultProps.

props.name: avatar
props.description: Optional leading avatar element (mutually exclusive with icon in practice).

props.name: onDelete
props.description: If set, shows a trailing delete icon and fires this on click/Backspace/Delete.

props.name: deleteIcon
props.description: Overrides the default delete icon. Only shown when onDelete is set.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Makes the chip clickable with hover/press feedback.

props.name: clickable
props.description: Forces the clickable visual treatment even without onClick, or suppresses it even with onClick.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the chip (renders at 38% opacity). Default false.

doThis: Use chips for filters/selections the user controls.
Keep status chips read-only with consistent colour + text mapping (never colour alone).
Make removable chips clearly removable via onDelete, not a custom close button.
dontDoThis: Don't use a chip as a primary button.
Don't encode status by colour alone.
Don't cram long text into a chip — keep labels short.
accessibility: Clickable chips are focusable with a clear role; MUI handles this automatically once onClick/clickable is set.
Removal via onDelete is keyboard-operable (Backspace/Delete when focused).
Status chips include text, never colour alone.
Touch/click targets meet minimum size — avoid overriding size down below 24px.

related.name: Button
related.why: Primary commands are buttons, not chips
